records.ety: [LL. defoliare, defoliatum, to shed leaves; L. de- + folium leaf: cf. F. dfoliation.]

defs: the act or process of causing plants to lose their leaves, especially by application of a chemical agent.
notes: The deliberate defoliation of plants has been used in war (as in Vietnam) to deprive an enemy of cover and allow attack from the air; also, to destroy narcotic-producing plants as a tactic against illegal drug production. The chemical defoliating agents are often sprayed over large areas from airplanes.
